title: kyllinga umbellata var. sumatrensis
intro: Kyllinga umbellata var. sumatrensis, also known as the Sumatran water fringe, is a type of grassy plant commonly found in wetlands and aquatic areas. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and fever. The health benefits of Kyllinga umbellata var. sumatrensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can be used in various forms of traditional medicine, but it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
